options.text: Please provide the naming components below. The Business Unit Identifier is optional and specifically used to uniquely identify resources and resource groups between business units in a shared AVD subscription. If the business unit identifier is not specified (left blank), then it is assumed the subscription delineates the business unit and therefore centralized monitoring and management is assumed and resources and resource groups are named accordingly.

elements.name: businessUnitIdentifier
elements.label: Business Unit Identifier
elements.toolTip: Input a 10 character max identifier for the business unit utilizing AVD in your tenant.

constraints.validationMessage: The value must be 1 - 8 characters in length and must be alphanumeric.

elements.name: hostPoolIdentifier
elements.label: HostPool Identifier
elements.toolTip: Input a 10 character max identifier for the hostpool created by this solution.

validations.message: [concat('The length cannot exceed ', string(sub(10, length(steps('basics').naming.businessUnitIdentifier))), ' characters, which equals 10 - length of the Business Unit Identifier.')]

elements.name: envShortName
elements.label: Environment
elements.toolTip: Select the target environment for the solution: Development (d), Test (t), Production (p). The abbreviation will be used as part of the naming convention for the resoure groups and resources.

elements.name: workSpace
elements.label: Workspace

options.text: This solution allows you to update an existing AVD Workspace and update the application group references or create a new Workspace. Select the appropriate option below.

Select an existing AVD Workspace below to which the Desktop Application Group will be registered. The Hostpool and Application Group metadata will be stored in the region where this workspace is located.

elements.existingFeedWorkspaceResourceId: [map(first(filter(steps('controlPlane').workspacesApi.value, (item) => and(startsWith(item.name, steps('basics').naming.identifier), and(contains(item.name, 'feed'), and(contains(item.name, steps('basics').naming.environment), equals(item.location, steps('controlPlane').controlPlane.location)))))), (item) => item.id)]

existingFeedWorkspaceResourceId.name: existingWorkspace
existingFeedWorkspaceResourceId.label: Existing AVD Workspace
existingFeedWorkspaceResourceId.toolTip: Select the existing AVD Workspace to update with the Desktop Application Group deployed via this solution.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(steps('controlPlane').workSpace.workspacesApi.value, (item) => parse(concat('{"label":"', item.name, '","description":"Location: ', item.location, ' Resource Group: ', first(skip(split(item.id, '/'), 4)), '","value":"', item.id, '"}')))]

elements.name: locationControlPlane
elements.label: Region (Control Plane)
elements.toolTip: Select the location for the AVD control plane resources: host pool, workspace, application group, etc.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('controlPlane').region.locationsApi.value, (item) => contains(first(map(filter(steps('controlPlane').region.providerApi.value, (item) => equals(item.resourceType, 'hostpools')), (item) => item.locations)), item.displayName)), (item) => parse(concat('{"label":"', item.displayName, '","value":"', item.name, '"}')))]

elements.name: workspaceFriendlyName
elements.label: Workspace Friendly Name
elements.toolTip: Input the friendly name for the AVD workspace that will be displayed in the end user's client.

constraints.validationMessage: The value must be between 1 and 64 characters in length.

elements.name: desktopFriendlyName
elements.label: Session Desktop Friendly Name
elements.toolTip: Input the friendly name for the default SessionDesktop that will be displayed in the end user's client.

constraints.validationMessage: The value must be between 1 and 20 characters in length.

options.text: Zero Trust is a new security model that assumes breach and verifies each request as though it originated from an uncontrolled network.

Private Endpoints are one key technology we can use to implement Zero Trust Architecture Guidance. Private endpoints should be utilized for all Platform as a Service (PaaS) resources (i.e., Automation Accounts, Blob Storage, File Storage, and Key Vaults utilized by this solution. Private Endpoints have their own set of prereqs including Private DNS Zones.

link.label: Microsoft Learn | Private Link Deployment in a Hub and Spoke Network

options.text:
When deploying private endpoints, you must select the subnet to which they will be attached and the Private DNS Zones to enable DNS resolution. Since this deployment expects that an Azure Landing Zone has already been deployed, you may be utilizing multiple subscriptions for different components of your architecture. To support this, the following drop downs allow you to select the subscription(s) containing the subnets and the Private DNS zones required to support Zero Trust. Once you select the subscription(s), you'll be able to select the virtual network, subnet, and appropropriate Private DNS Zones from these subscriptions. The following Private DNS Zones may be required:
  • Azure Files for FSLogix profile storage (privatelink.file.suffix)
  • Azure Key Vault (privatelink.vaultcore.suffix)
  • Automation Accounts for the Storage Auto Quota Increase capability (privatelink.azure-automation.suffix)
  • Azure Blob Storage for Disk Access and artifacts storage (privatelink.blob.suffix)
See the documentation in the source repo to determine the correct fully-qualified domain name for each zone based on the cloud.

elements.name: virtualNetwork
elements.label: Virtual Network
elements.toolTip: Select the virtual network containing the subnets to which the management resource and storage account (if applicable) private endpoints will be attached.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('zeroTrust').privateEndpoints.virtualNetworksApi.value, (item) => equals(item.location, steps('basics').scope.location.name)), (item) => parse(concat('{"label":"', item.name, '","description":"Location: ', item.location, ' Resource Group: ', first(skip(split(item.id, '/'), 4)), '","value":"', item.id, '"}')))]

elements.name: subnet
elements.label: Subnet
elements.toolTip: Select an existing subnet for the private Endpoints.

elements.name: privateDNSZonesSubscription
elements.label: Private DNS Zone Subscription
elements.toolTip: Select the subscription that contains your Private DNS Zones.

elements.name: automationAccountPrivateDnsZone
elements.label: Automation Account Private DNS Zone
elements.toolTip: Select the existing Private DNS Zone for automation accounts.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('zeroTrust').privateEndpoints.privateDNSZonesApi.value, (item) => contains(item.name, 'privatelink.azure-automation.')), (item) => parse(concat('{"label":"', item.name, '","description":"Resource group: ', first(skip(split(item.id, '/'), 4)), '","value":"', item.id, '"}')))]

elements.name: azureBlobsPrivateDnsZone
elements.label: Azure Blob Storage Private DNS Zone
elements.toolTip: Select the existing Private DNS Zone for Azure Blob Storage.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('zeroTrust').privateEndpoints.privateDNSZonesApi.value, (item) => contains(item.name, 'privatelink.blob.')), (item) => parse(concat('{"label":"', item.name, '","description":"Resource group: ', first(skip(split(item.id, '/'), 4)), '","value":"', item.id, '"}')))]

elements.name: azureFilesPrivateDnsZone
elements.label: Azure Files Private DNS Zone
elements.toolTip: Select the existing Private DNS Zone for Azure Files.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('zeroTrust').privateEndpoints.privateDNSZonesApi.value, (item) => contains(item.name, 'privatelink.file.')), (item) => parse(concat('{"label":"', item.name, '","description":"Resource group: ', first(skip(split(item.id, '/'), 4)), '","value":"', item.id, '"}')))]

elements.name: keyVaultPrivateDnsZone
elements.label: Azure Key Vault Private DNS Zone
elements.toolTip: Select the existing Private DNS Zone for Azure Key Vaults.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('zeroTrust').privateEndpoints.privateDNSZonesApi.value, (item) => contains(item.name, 'privatelink.vaultcore.')), (item) => parse(concat('{"label":"', item.name, '","description":"Resource group: ', first(skip(split(item.id, '/'), 4)), '","value":"', item.id, '"}')))]

link.label: Microsoft Learn | AVD Private Link Overview

options.text: You can use Azure Private Link with Azure Virtual Desktop to privately connect to your remote resources. By creating a private endpoint, traffic between your virtual network and the service remains on the Microsoft network, so you no longer need to expose your service to the public internet. You also use a VPN or ExpressRoute for your users with the Remote Desktop client to connect to the virtual network. Keeping traffic within the Microsoft network improves security and keeps your data safe.

link.label: Microsoft Learn | AVD Private Link Overview

options.text:
When enabling Private Link with Azure Virtual Desktop, you have the following supported scenarios to connect to Azure Virtual Desktop. Choose the configuration below to meet your requirements.
  1. All parts of the connection - initial feed discovery, feed download, and remote session connections - use private routes.

  2. Feed download and remote session connections use private routes, but initial feed discovery uses public routes.

  3. Only remote session connections use private routes, but initial feed discovery and feed download use public routes.

elements.name: avdPrivateLinkConfig
elements.label: AVD Private Link Configuration
elements.defaultValue: [if(equals(steps('controlPlane').workSpace.createOption, 'new'), 'All parts of the connection use private routes', 'Only remote session connections use private routes')]
elements.toolTip: Chooose the AVD Private Link Configuration.

allowedValues.label: All parts of the connection use private routes
allowedValues.description: Global feed, Workspace feed, and hostpool private endpoints are required

allowedValues.label: Feed download and remote session connections use private routes
allowedValues.description: Workspace feed and Hostpool private endpoints are required

allowedValues.label: Only remote session connections use private routes

options.text: Select the subscription, virtual network, and subnet for the remote session connection private endpoint
Select the subscription, virtual network, and subnet for the remote session connection and Workspace Feed private endpoints

elements.name: cpVirtualNetwork
elements.label: Virtual Network
elements.toolTip: Select the virtual network where the AVD Private Link Hostpool Connection and Workspace Feed (If applicable) Private Endpoints will be created. Note that only virtual networks located in the same region as the control plane are shown.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('zeroTrust').avdPrivateLink.virtualNetworksApi.value, (item) => equals(item.location, steps('basics').scope.location.name)), (item) => parse(concat('{"label":"', item.name, '","description":"Location: ', item.location, ' Resource group: ', first(skip(split(item.id, '/'), 4)), '","value":"', item.id, '"}')))]

elements.name: cpSubnet
elements.label: Subnet
elements.toolTip: Select an existing subnet for the private Endpoints.

elements.name: wvdPrivateLinkDnsZone
elements.label: AVD Private DNS Zone
elements.toolTip: Select the existing Private DNS Zone for the Remote Session connection and Workspace Feed.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('zeroTrust').avdPrivateLink.privateDNSZonesApi.value, (item) => contains(item.name, 'privatelink.wvd.')), (item) => parse(concat('{"label":"', item.name, '","description":"Resource group: ', first(skip(split(item.id, '/'), 4)), '","value":"', item.id, '"}')))]

elements.name: hpPublicNetworkAccess
elements.label: Remote Session Connection public access
elements.toolTip: Select the appropriate option for configuring public network access to the remote session.

allowedValues.label: Disabled
allowedValues.description: Remote sessions are denied when either the client or session host is using a public route.

allowedValues.label: Enabled
allowedValues.description: Remote sessions are allowed when either the client or session host are using a private or public route.

allowedValues.label: Enabled For Clients Only
allowedValues.description: Remote sessions are allowed as long as the session host is using a private route, regardless of the route the client is using.

elements.name: feedDownloadPublicNetworkAccess
elements.label: Workspace feed public access
elements.defaultValue: Public access enabled from all networks

allowedValues.label: Public access enabled from all networks
allowedValues.description: Workspace feed requests are allowed from private and public routes.

allowedValues.label: Public access disabled from all networks
allowedValues.description: Workspace feed requests are allowed only from private routes.
